narrative.headline: 2022 Jeep Grand Cherokee: electrical problems lead the complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 351 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is by far the most reported area at 46%, more than twice any other, followed by the engine (19%) and forward collision avoidance (17%). No fires, injuries, or deaths are tied to the top areas except forward collision avoidance, which is linked to 2 injuries and 3 crashes. Engine and forward collision avoidance each have 3 crashes, the most of any area.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: By far the biggest complaint: 161 reports (46%), with 1 crash. No fires or injuries reported here, but the sheer volume makes this the area to scrutinize.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 67 reports (19%), tied to 3 crashes (the most of any area), with no fires or injuries. Worth a careful check on a test drive.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 61 reports (17%) and the most serious by outcome: 3 crashes and 2 injuries, the only top area linked to injuries. Several owners describe phantom braking.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 53 reports (15%), with 1 crash and no fires or injuries. A recurring complaint area worth keeping in mind.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 51 reports (15%), linked to 2 crashes and no fires or injuries. Several reports come from owners of the 4Xe hybrid powertrain.
